fields.description:
## Context

Observed 2026-07-10 during the adept-hedge-stain epic close-out: `rebar fsck` reported 14
issues (later 17 after more write activity) — MISSING_CREATE + ORPHAN_EVENT
('pre-snapshot event not captured in source_event_uuids'). This ticket's original open
question was whether those were (a) false positives from binary/store version skew (an older
binary misreading newer ENQUEUE_ENRICH-era events) or (b) genuine faults.

## RESOLVED (2026-07-11)

The ENQUEUE_ENRICH-introducing change has **already merged to main** (current binary = main
2d1524ad8; `rebar fsck` prints `ensures: 5/5 applied` with NO "store contains events newer
than binary" WARN), so AC1's gate is satisfied and the classification can be made from the
current binary. Re-running fsck from that binary, the faults **persist** — so they are **NOT
version-skew false positives**. They are real, but BENIGN, artifacts (full forensic
classification recorded as a comment on this ticket and on 115b):

- **13 ORPHAN_EVENT** — benign snapshot-staleness from a distributed compaction-vs-reconciler
  write race (the Jira reconciler writes an assignee EDIT; a compaction in a different clone
  snapshots before observing it; the reconciler re-emits an identical EDIT after the snapshot
  which drives live state). Live status/assignee verified correct for all 13.
- **4 MISSING_CREATE** — local-only phantom dirs (0 tracked files, not in HEAD, no structural
  refs); 110e is a local duplicate of the tracked epic 6d0d.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Re-run `rebar fsck` with the ENQUEUE_ENRICH-era binary against the live store and record
  the counts on this ticket. (DONE: 13 ORPHAN_EVENT + 4 MISSING_CREATE, `ensures: 5/5 applied`.)
- [ ] Classify each fault as version-skew artifact vs real. (DONE: none are version-skew — they
  persist under the current binary; classified real-but-benign per the forensic comment.)
- [ ] Remediate the real faults with A3-safe tooling (NOT `fsck --repair-snapshots`, which
  rebuilds from the full log and previously corrupted 11/13 statuses): retire each orphan EDIT
  file (`.json` -> `.json.retired`, which fsck skips) and delete the 4 local phantom dirs, then
  confirm `rebar fsck` reports zero ORPHAN_EVENT + zero MISSING_CREATE with no ticket's
  status/assignee changed. (This is the shared remediation tracked jointly with 115b.)

## Notes

- Root cause of the recurring orphan race (compaction snapshotting before observing a remote
  reconciler write) is a separate durable-fix follow-up, out of scope for this classify-and-
  remediate-to-zero ticket.
